articles.title: This $99 project management tool keeps tasks, docs, and sprints in one place for up to 10 users for life
articles.description: TL;DR: Lyra Project Management is an all-in-one team workspace with unlimited projects and lifetime access for up to 10 users, now only $99 (reg. $1,054). Most project management setups involve at least two or three tools that don’t talk to each other cleanly. Lyra puts tasks, documentation, sprint planning, and analytics in one place, and a lifetime license for up to 10 users is only $99 right now (reg. $1,054). Using Lyra is simple. Teams organize work through issues and sub-issues with priorities, statuses, and assignees. From there, you can group tasks into cycles and sprints for time-boxed planning, or bundle related work into modules and epics with clear status tracking. Custom workflows let you adapt task stages to match how your team actually operates. Views include Kanban boards, Gantt charts, calendar, list, and spreadsheet layouts, all updating in real time across every user on the account. Pages handle documentation, notes, and internal wikis without needing a separate tool. Intake Queues convert incoming requests directly into trackable tasks, and integrations cover Slack, GitHub, and webhooks. An AI workspace assistant even handles generating, summarizing, and organizing content to speed up daily output. The lifetime plan covers unlimited projects, advanced analytics, priority support, and all future updates with no recurring fees. Get a Lyra Project Management Unlimited Plan Lifetime Subscription for $99. Lyra Project Management Unlimited Plan (10 Users): Lifetime Subscription See Deal StackSocial prices subject to change.

articles.title: EVE Online's Cradle of War expansion wants to make the space MMO more welcoming to new players, before killing them in galaxy-wide omniconflict
articles.description: The effective onboarding of curious yet noncommitted first-time EVE Online players is a conundrum that developers Fenris Creations, formerly CCP Games , have been trying to solve for decades. Many are enticed by the MMORPG’s purely player-spun tales of espionage and military adventure , only to bounce off its dense hull of complex economic spacemaths and a sandbox occupied by oft-unpunished pirates and con artists. Perhaps the game’s next big expansion, Cradle of War, will have more luck when it launches on June 9th. Read more
